articles.headline: SoftBank unit plans debut euro bond as group accelerates AI investments
articles.summary: SoftBank's mobile unit is arranging its first euro-denominated bond with 6- and 10-year maturities to fund Masayoshi Son's major AI push, following a successful $1B US bond. The group explores $40B in loans for US tech investments. This financing signals aggressive capital raises for AI, impacting global tech funding and competition.

articles.headline: Foxconn rides AI server demand as first-quarter revenue jumps 29.7%
articles.summary: Foxconn's Q1 revenue surged 29.7% year-over-year, driven by strong AI server and cloud networking demand, signaling robust hyperscaler spending flowing through the supply chain. March revenue hit a record 45.6% increase, but geopolitical risks like Middle East conflict may impact visibility. This indicates sustained AI infrastructure demand benefiting manufacturers beyond chip designers.

articles.headline: $10 Billion in Savings, 30,000 Jobs Lost: Oracle Goes All-In on AI
articles.summary: Oracle is aggressively expanding data-center spending while absorbing new debt to compete in AI infrastructure, resulting in 30,000 job losses. The company's stock has dropped over 50% from September 2025 highs, signaling market concerns about capital intensity and execution risks in the AI race.
